scenario.title: Refactoring a Node.js Service with Failing Tests and Performance Regressions
scenario.user_goal: Complete the refactor, make all tests pass, and ensure the updated endpoint maintains or improves performance while preserving backward-compatible response behavior.

initial_observation.events:
[09:35:05] User runs the full test suite locally, captures failing test names and stack traces, and identifies response-shape mismatches in controller/serializer code paths (e.g., missing/renamed fields in JSON responses).
[09:35:50] User opens the relevant endpoint/controller and serializer implementations for the failing cases and compares current response payloads against the expected backward-compatible schema asserted by the tests.
[09:36:10] User re-runs the targeted failing tests and encounters an additional unexpected failure due to an error-response contract mismatch (tests expect a specific {code, message} structure but the implementation returns a different shape), prompting further adjustment of the serializer/error mapping.
initial_observation.scenario: A developer is refactoring a Node.js/TypeScript backend API that powers an internal dashboard. They start by cleaning up a legacy controller and adding a new query filter. Midway through, the test suite begins failing due to a subtle change in error handling and a mismatch between expected response shapes. After fixing the tests, they notice a performance regression in one endpoint and suspect inefficient database queries. Throughout the work, a proactive AI agent could help by anticipating likely bottlenecks (e.g., missing indexes, N+1 queries), suggesting safe refactors, drafting targeted test cases, and performing quick static checks (lint/type errors, edge-case coverage) before the developer hits runtime errors. The agent should also watch for context like version control diffs, failing stack traces, and patterns in code changes to offer timely, actionable suggestions rather than generic advice.
initial_observation.goal: Complete the refactor, make all tests pass, and ensure the updated endpoint maintains or improves performance while preserving backward-compatible response behavior.

observation.events:
[09:35:05] User runs the full test suite locally, captures failing test names and stack traces, and identifies response-shape mismatches in controller/serializer code paths (e.g., missing/renamed fields in JSON responses).
[09:35:50] User opens the relevant endpoint/controller and serializer implementations for the failing cases and compares current response payloads against the expected backward-compatible schema asserted by the tests.
[09:36:10] User re-runs the targeted failing tests and encounters an additional unexpected failure due to an error-response contract mismatch (tests expect a specific {code, message} structure but the implementation returns a different shape), prompting further adjustment of the serializer/error mapping.
[09:36:20] User patches the endpoint controller/serializer layer to restore the legacy JSON success-payload schema by aligning field names/renamed keys with what the tests assert.
[09:36:35] User unifies error handling in the controller/serializer so all error responses match the exact expected {code, message} structure, including mapping internal exceptions to the correct external error codes.
[09:36:55] User re-runs the previously failing targeted tests to confirm the response-shape and error-contract fixes, then prepares to run the full suite after the targeted failures are resolved.
observation.feedback: You correctly stayed silent. The user didn't need interruption.

steps.action: [predict] Help user set up the development environment

[09:36:56] User runs the full test suite again and confirms whether the previously fixed response-shape and error-contract mismatches remain resolved; if any failures persist, the user inspects the specific failing test(s) and traces the controller/serializer mapping for the expected legacy {code, message} structure.

judgment_history.task: Help user set up the development environment
judgment_history.thought: The user’s observation shows they are actively debugging and patching controller/serializer response and error contract schemas, and then re-running targeted tests. The proposed task—helping set up the development environment—doesn’t directly address the immediate work shown (fixing response-shape and error-contract mismatches). Unless the user explicitly indicates environment setup is blocking them, this would be an unnecessary interruption.

action_history: [predict] Help user set up the development environment
